entities.name: PARALONGIDORUS BULLATUS
entities.description: A new species of nematode described from groundnut soils in Niger, characterized by specific body and odontostyle lengths, a knob-like head, and a digitate tail ending.

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop whose soils in Niger were sampled for the discovery of Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: SADOR6
entities.description: A specific location in Niger where Paralongidorus bullatus was found.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted and the specimens were collected.

entities.name: BODY LENGTH
entities.description: A morphological measurement of the nematode, specifically 4.4-5.5 mm for the female of Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: ODONTOSTYLE LENGTH
entities.description: A morphological measurement of the nematode, specifically 132-156 micrometers for Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: KNOB-LIKE HEAD
entities.description: A distinguishing morphological feature of the head of Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: DIGITATE ENDING OF THE PROTOPLASMIC REGION OF THE TAIL
entities.description: A distinguishing morphological feature of the tail of Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: TAIL TERMINUS
entities.description: The end part of the tail, described as conoid to broadly rounded in Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: UTERI
entities.description: Reproductive organs described as well developed and without sperms in Paralongidorus bullatus.

entities.name: XIPHINEMA PARASETARIAE
entities.description: A nematode species validated in the study and proposed as the senior synonym for X. attorodorum.

entities.name: XIPHINEMA ATTORODORUM
entities.description: A nematode species proposed as a synonym of Xiphinema parasetariae based on measurements and tail structures.

entities.name: MEASUREMENTS
entities.description: The process of quantifying morphological traits used to describe species and validate taxonomic synonyms.

relationships.target: DIGITATE ENDING OF THE PROTOPLASMIC REGION OF THE TAIL
